memory.summary: The group is leaving soon for Echo, but my midterm is tomorrow and I still need a couple of hours to finish reviewing.
I've successfully declined Mina's invitation to study tonight, prioritizing my midterm review over the group trip to Echo.
I've made significant progress on my midterm review, but I missed the window to go out with friends. My energy is still decent, and focusing on studying was the right choice.
Studying for 1.5 hours boosted my midterm progress to 0.89, confirming that prioritizing the exam over the trip was effective despite my fatigue.
I successfully declined Mina's study invitation to focus on my midterm, and the extra review time has been effective despite my fatigue.
My midterm preparation is now complete, eliminating the primary source of stress. I can finally rest and recover from my sleep debt.
I am fully rested with zero sleep debt, and my midterm preparation is complete, so I am ready to begin the exam.
